mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates weakening labor markets, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when the rate exceeds 5.5%.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the INDPRO-GDPC1 composite falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a tightening monetary environment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns during monetary tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS exceed thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through increased construction and related sector activity.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 40% of instances where HOUST exceeds its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during supply shocks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO declines by 3% or more.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates a resilient labor market and accommodative policy, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market strength with monetary easing
failure_condition: Rank IC for the PAYEMS-FEDFUNDS interaction falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining consumer spending (RSAFS) during recessionary regimes signals weak demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS declines by 2% or more.
